mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator simplifies the deployment and management of RabbitMQ clusters on Kubernetes. It automates tasks such as cluster creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to manage RabbitMQ clusters efficiently within Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://charts.rabbitmq.com

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the RabbitMQ Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of RabbitMQ clusters.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.19.2 --namespace rabbitmq-system --create-namespace

```

> Installs the operator in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Applies the sample RabbitMQ cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Lists all pods in the RabbitMQ namespace to verify their status.
> Checks the status of the RabbitMQ cluster custom resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if the RabbitMQ cluster is not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq-system

```

> Uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.19.2 --namespace rabbitmq-system

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system

```

> Backs up the current RabbitMQ cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rabbitmqclusters -n rabbitmq-system -o yaml > backup-rabbitmqclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the required permissions are not set for the operator.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the operator has the necessary RBAC permissions to create resources.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RabbitMQ configuration is incorrect or if there are insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq-system' for details and adjust the configuration or resources accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: RabbitMQ cluster is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Network policies or service configurations may be blocking access.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and network policies to ensure they allow traffic to the RabbitMQ service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RabbitMQ cluster has not been created or was deleted.

**Fix:**
Reapply the RabbitMQ cluster configuration using 'kubectl apply -f <cluster-config-file>'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved handling of RabbitMQ cluster upgrades and added support for custom resource validation.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq-system'. The RabbitMQ cluster custom resource should also show a status of 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
